label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,W,W,L : value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE, naive BLOCKS, cmplog non-decisive (_). The only technique delta between the involved winner (vp/vpc) and loser (naive) is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (naive has edge-only feedback). The shape's Layer-1 attribution is therefore VP CMP_MAP gradient; this test is the INDEPENDENT real-data verification (G2) of HOW that gradient advantage manifests in the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING corpus content.

round_history.round0: Two MEASUREMENT MODES of one gradient claim: vp_cmpmap_gradient_distance_closure (value_distance_reached) validated 5 fixed-operand branches (lcms x4 + sqlite3/14408); vp_cmpmap_gradient_depth_reach was an UNBUILT seed_set parse-proxy that never scored 4 branches. Distance-by-proxy DEAD for the inconclusive 9.
round_history.round2: Pivoted to TOKEN DENSITY (vp_admits_token_dense_corpus, struct token-per-byte) which confirmed 3 SQL grammar branches, plus vp_admits_larger_valid_structure (seed bigness, font only) which confirmed 2 (harfbuzz). After round-2 arbitration 10 branches are CONFIRMED+FROZEN; 4 remained rule_not_met (mechanism not supported by the round-2 measurements).
round_history.round3: Added vp_cmpmap_iteration_depth_reach (BUILT depth_reach) for the 4 round-2 rule_not_met branches (range/loop/deep-dispatch gates with no fixed operand). depth_reach CONFIRMED 3 of them (libxml2/6429 hex-loop, libxml2/6467_h1 digit-run-past-20, harfbuzz/6616 deep codepoint switch) -- genuine iteration/parse-depth gates. ONE branch, libxml2/6461, scored rule_not_met under depth_reach (and is N/A under distance/density/size): see round3_final below.
round_history.round3_final: libxml2/6461 is structurally UNLIKE the other three round-3 H4 members. Its gate (parser.c:2317 NXT(2)=='x', operand 0x78, width 1) is a SINGLE-BYTE EQUALITY to a fixed operand with NO iteration depth -- past 'x' the hex-charref parse is a fixed-length consumption, not a counter-growing loop -- so depth_reach correctly did not separate it (the 'depth' difference is really a did-you-reach-the-line side-count, established non-discriminating in round 3). It is ALSO a FLOATING operand (the '&#x' opener appears anywhere in an XML document, not at a fixed file-head offset), so operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) returns no_gate_signature, and at width 1 value_distance_reached collapses to match/no-match (the registry flags width<=2 as chance-alignment prone, and a real gradient is indistinguishable from naive's 1/10 lucky hit). EVERY built measurement is therefore either already-refuted-for-this-branch (H1 width-1 chance, H2 not-grammar-density, H3 not-font-size, H4 no-depth) or structurally blind (operand_enrichment needs a fixed offset the floating charref opener lacks). The genuine mechanism (single-byte Hamming gradient to 0x78) is real but UNMEASURABLE by any built tool without over-claiming -> honest decidable:false (H6).

round0_outcome.validated_via: vp_cmpmap_distance_closure_fixed_operand (value_distance_reached: naive_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5) -- FIXED-operand value gates (lcms FOURCCs, sqlite3 '||'); CARRIED FORWARD unchanged so the round-3 pivot does NOT regress them.

hypotheses.label: For gates with a SINGLE fixed comparison operand, value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a corpus that lands at a strictly smaller residual Hamming/prefix distance to that operand than the naive-blocking corpus ever reaches. (CARRIED FORWARD; already validated for 5 branches -- kept to avoid regression.)

params.note: Operand from signature.operand_literal + operand_width_bytes. Run by whichever server owns the corpus (lcms on sB; sqlite3 on s4). All 5 HELD this in round-0; carried forward verbatim.

hypotheses.prediction: naive_min_distance > 0 (the naive-blocking corpus systematically STALLS at a positive residual to the fixed operand -- carries 'RGB ' not the FOURCC, no '||') while value_profile closes it, so distance_closure_ratio is large. DISCRIMINATING vs a no-gradient null (VP got the operand by the same blind luck as naive), which predicts naive_min_distance ~ vp_min_distance.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a token-DENSE corpus: the VP-resolving seed set carries far more of the gate's required grammar/structural tokens PER BYTE than the naive-blocking set, because the gradient rewards concentrated partial-assembly and prunes bloat, whereas naive's edge-only feedback accumulates large, token-SPARSE byte-garbage. (CARRIED FORWARD; validated round-2 for the SQL-grammar subset.)

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT joint_necessity struct compute (seed_bytes loader + struct_stats + analyze_pair winner=value_profile/loser=naive) but EMITS one new metric: token_density = mean over a fuzzer's seeds of (count of --tokens present) / (seed length in bytes). token_density_lift = vp_token_density / naive_token_density. registry_tool=null -> orchestrator parameterizes the existing seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch descriptor to emit density (not a new file).

tokens_by_branch.sqlite3/14588: SELECT,FROM,*,<<,%,(,),- -- deep arithmetic-expression operators over numeric literals.

params.scope: ON-DISK CORPUS REQUIRED (s4 holds sqlite3). Needs >=3 value_profile resolving + >=3 naive blocking seeds; sqlite3/14745_h0 with VP W=2 may fall to seed_starved (H5).

hypotheses.prediction: token_density_lift >= 2.0 (VP corpus carries at least 2x more grammar tokens per byte than naive). DISCRIMINATING vs the refuted scalar-distance story (density counts whole-token PRESENCE per byte, orthogonal to residual Hamming distance), vs the falsified bigness story (here VP seeds are SMALLER), and vs a no-gradient null (token_density_lift ~ 1.0).

hypotheses.label: For STRUCTURAL FORMAT-INTEGRITY gates (sfnt/font assembly), value_profile's gradient admits a corpus of PHYSICALLY LARGER, valid-header seeds than the naive-blocking corpus -- the gradient toward well-formed table directories produces bigger, more-tokened inputs. (CARRIED FORWARD; validated round-2 for harfbuzz/5302.)

measurement.tool_mode: analyze_pair(winner_fz=value_profile, loser_fz=naive) -- __WWL has value_profile RESOLVING and naive BLOCKING; the existing analyze_pair emits winner_/loser_/tag_lift/size_lift, no code change.

params.scope: harfbuzz on-disk on s4; W=value_profile(10) L=naive(10). Round-2 confirmed size_lift=4.69, tag_lift=1.0.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.3 AND tag_lift >= 1.0 : the VP-resolving font corpus is >=30% larger AND carries >=1 more valid sfnt table tag than the naive-blocking corpus. DISCRIMINATING: a no-gradient null predicts size_lift ~ 1.0 AND tag_lift ~ 0; the SQL/grammar branches FALSIFY the bigness direction (VP smaller), so this rule is the FONT-assembly signature, not a generic 'VP bigger'.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round-3 pivot). For RANGE / LOOP / DEEP-DISPATCH gates with NO harvestable single fixed-byte operand (digit-range loops past a counter threshold, a hex-charref loop reaching its non-hex terminator, a deep codepoint switch arm), value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits a corpus that drives the gate's branch LINE to a materially greater per-seed EXECUTION DEPTH than the naive-blocking corpus, which stalls shallow. The gradient is visible NOT as residual operand distance (no fixed operand) nor token-per-byte density nor seed size, but as how DEEP / how many TIMES the average winner seed drives the branch line through the instrumented binary.
replaces: vp_cmpmap_gradient_depth_reach (round-0 UNBUILT seed_set parse-proxy -- now realized by the BUILT depth_reach Docker/llvm-cov tool)

measurement.tool_mode: branch(--winner-fuzzer value_profile --loser-fuzzer naive) -- runs the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING seeds through libafl-<target>-cov + per_role_in_container.py, reads the branch line's per-seed-normalized llvm-cov execution count. Needs >=1 W and >=1 L on-disk seed (NOT >=3 -- depth normalizes per seed), so it scores the seed-starved harfbuzz/6616 (VP W=2) that distance/density/size could not.

params.rule_metric_note: Rule keys on depth_lift (per-seed line-execution depth ratio) + winner_deeper, NOT on side_lift. Smoke on harfbuzz/6616 showed side_lift=0.91 (the True/False arm count at a SWITCH dispatch line is noisy and NON-discriminating), while depth_lift=3.0 / winner_deeper=true cleanly separates -- so the decision rule must use line-execution depth, not the blocked-side count, for switch/loop gates. round3 NOTE: this depth signal is genuine ONLY for gates with real ITERATION/PARSE depth (loop counters, hex loop to terminator, deep case dispatch); a single-byte equality gate with NO iteration past it (libxml2/6461) is NOT a member -- its lack of depth correctly scored rule_not_met and it is moved to the honest decidable:false residue H6, NOT relaxed into this rule.

depth_proxy_by_branch.libxml2/6429: branch line in xmlParseCharRef hex loop (the else->xmlFatalErr arm / case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF): VP corpus drives the hex-digit loop deep enough to present a non-hex terminator; naive's '<?xml ..?>' docs never enter the loop. sB-owned corpus.
depth_proxy_by_branch.libxml2/6467_h1: line 2353 'if (count++ > 20)' inside the decimal digit-run loop: VP corpus grows long in-range digit runs (analysis: W branch hitcount 8 vs L 1) so the loop LINE is executed many more times per seed and the reset branch trips; naive enters the loop only ~4 times and exits shallow. sB-owned corpus.
depth_proxy_by_branch.harfbuzz/6616: line 309 'case 0x0D91u' codepoint switch in the vowel-constraints shaper: VP corpus drives the shaper to reach line 309 (smoke: winner_depth=1.5 vs loser_depth=0.17, depth_lift=3.0, winner_deeper=true) -- the value_profile seeds carry the Sinhala-script bytes that reach the deep case dispatch, naive's garbage shapes to a buffer that never reaches it. s4-owned corpus; scored on s4 despite VP W=2 (depth_reach needs only >=1 W/L).

params.scope: Docker per-branch (~30-60s) against libafl-<target>-cov. harfbuzz/6616 scores on s4 (cov image present, W=2/L=6 verified). libxml2/6429 and libxml2/6467_h1 are sB-owned (libafl-libxml2-cov + libxml2 seeds live on sB; Dockerfile.libxml2.cov exists) -- they are designed-valid and ARBITRATED BY sB, NOT marked decidable:false merely because absent on s4 (re-design rule #4). libxml2/6461 is NO LONGER a member of this hypothesis (moved to H6 decidable:false).

hypotheses.prediction: winner_depth > loser_depth with depth_lift >= 1.5 : the value_profile-resolving corpus drives the gate's branch line to a materially greater per-seed execution depth than the naive-blocking corpus, which stalls shallow (naive's digit run never reaches the loop-counter threshold; naive's input never reaches the hex loop terminator / the deep case arm). DISCRIMINATING on TWO fronts: (1) vs the prior-3 measurements which are STRUCTURALLY BLIND here -- there is no single fixed operand (distance H1 N/A / chance-prone at width<=2), no token-density separation (H2 refuted for these), and no font-size signature (H3 font-only); depth captures the CUMULATIVE iteration/parse gradient those scalar/byte measurements cannot. (2) vs a no-gradient null (VP got lucky on the final byte, the shape label is noise), which predicts depth_lift ~ 1.0 (both corpora reach the line equally shallow); only a real CMP_MAP gradient that admits progressively deeper in-range/in-grammar seeds predicts VP's corpus is systematically DEEPER per seed. Smoke harfbuzz/6616 -> depth_lift=3.0, winner_deeper=true -> CONFIRMED. libxml2/6429 + libxml2/6467_h1 expected to HOLD on sB (loop/range gates).

hypotheses.label: NEW (round-3 final). honest non-discriminable residue for a SINGLE-BYTE EQUALITY gate to a FIXED operand that FLOATS in the input (no fixed file-head offset). value_profile's CMP_MAP does write a Hamming-distance bucket on the 1-byte compare -- the mechanism is genuinely a VP byte gradient -- but NO built tool can independently CONFIRM it without over-claiming: (a) value_distance_reached collapses at width 1 to match/no-match, where a real gradient is indistinguishable from naive's lucky 1/10 hit (registry flags width<=2 as chance-alignment prone; a relaxed re-test is forbidden); (b) depth_reach finds no separation because a single-byte equality has NO iteration depth past it (the hex parse downstream is fixed-length, not a counter-growing loop) -- it correctly scored rule_not_met; (c) operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) requires a FIXED-OFFSET gate signature, but the '&#x' charref opener appears at ANY position in an XML document, so gate_signature returns no_gate_signature; (d) token-density (H2) and seed-size (H3) are the wrong axes (one literal byte is not grammar-token density nor a size signature). The mechanism is real but UNMEASURABLE by the built toolset -> honest decidable:false.

params.note: libxml2/6461 (parser.c:2317, NXT(2)=='x', operand 0x78, width 1). FLOATING single-byte equality. Distinct CAUSE from the seed-starved residue H5 (which is >=3-winner starvation): here the VP arm is NOT seed-starved -- the obstruction is that EVERY built measurement is either already-refuted-for-this-branch (H1 width-1 chance, H4 no-depth, H2 not-density, H3 not-size) or structurally blind (operand_enrichment needs a fixed offset the floating opener lacks). This is the correct G1/G3 outcome: a single-byte Hamming gradient is genuinely real but cannot be discriminated from blind luck by any real-data tool we have, so we do NOT invent a rule to force a label. Would become decidable ONLY by a new tool not in the registry (e.g. a corpus-wide population-enrichment of byte 0x78 at the post-'&#' position, accounting for the floating offset) -- out of scope for this round. sB-owned (libxml2 corpus + libafl-libxml2-cov on sB); the s4 DB's branch_id 6461 is a DIFFERENT target's branch (cross-target collision), so even s4 cannot probe it. Honestly left inconclusive (fallback).

hypotheses.prediction: N/A -- no built measurement can discriminate a real single-byte (width-1) VP gradient from a blind-luck null at a floating operand. An honest inconclusive beats a rule the data cannot support.
hypotheses.rule: decidable:false -> arbiter leaves libxml2/6461 inconclusive (fallback). It would re-enter a decidable hypothesis ONLY if a NEW registry tool is built that measures corpus-wide population enrichment of the fixed byte at the floating post-'&#' offset (a fixed-offset-agnostic byte_freq), which the current toolset lacks.

hypotheses.label: honest non-discriminable residue: branches whose value_profile-RESOLVING arm has too few on-disk seeds for the applicable seed/struct tool to compute a stable median (token-density H2 needs >=3 W), AND that have no fixed operand (H1 N/A) nor font-size signature (H3) -- so no BUILT measurement can independently confirm VP's mechanism without over-claiming. (depth_reach H4 only needs >=1 W, so a branch is in this residue ONLY if even its depth signal is absent/unscorable.)

params.note: sqlite3/14658 (VP W=2) and sqlite3/14745_h0 (VP W=2) are seed-starved for the value_profile arm under H2's >=3 requirement. They are SQL deep-parse / wildcard gates: candidate for H4 depth_reach (needs only >=1 W) on a future pass IF a rank-10 re-bisect or the depth signal separates; until then they are honestly left inconclusive. They are NOT marked decidable:false for corpus-absence (their corpus is on-disk on s4) -- they are seed-STARVED for the chosen measurement, the correct G3 outcome. harfbuzz/6616 MOVED OUT of this residue into H4 (depth_reach scores it at W=2).

hypotheses.prediction: N/A -- the applicable seed/struct measurement lacks the >=3 winner seeds to compute a stable statistic. An honest inconclusive beats a rule the data cannot support.
hypotheses.rule: decidable:false -> arbiter leaves these inconclusive (fallback) unless a seed re-bisect raises the VP arm to >=3 (re-enters H2) or H4 depth_reach is run for them and separates (depth_lift>=1.5 AND winner_deeper).

data_realities_respected: value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> NEVER measured via a seed_lineage 'gradient event'. All four decidable hypotheses read what the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING corpus CONTAINS or REACHES: residual operand distance (H1), token density (H2), structural size+tags (H3), and per-seed branch-line execution DEPTH (H4, depth_reach runs the corpus through the cov binary and reads how deep the AVERAGE seed drives the gate line -- corpus-reach, not a lineage event) (data_realities #1,#2).
I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence proposed.

joint/structural-assembly gates have DIFFERENT-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds -> byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is NOT used as a decidable test: round-2/3 confirmed enrichment returns no_gate_signature for these range/loop gates, and for libxml2/6461 the '&#x' operand FLOATS (no fixed-offset gate signature) so operand_enrichment is structurally blind -- this is WHY libxml2/6461 is honest decidable:false (H6) rather than forced into an enrichment rule (data_realities #3).

on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3 ON s4; sB holds lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty. H1 runs on the corpus owner (lcms on sB, sqlite3 on s4). H2/H3 read on-disk seeds (sqlite3+harfbuzz on s4). H4 depth_reach needs libafl-<target>-cov + on-disk seeds: harfbuzz/6616 on s4 (image present, W=2/L=6 verified, smoke depth_lift=3.0 CONFIRMED); libxml2/6429 + libxml2/6467_h1 are sB-owned -> designed-valid and ARBITRATED BY sB (Dockerfile.libxml2.cov exists), NOT decidable:false for s4-absence (re-design rule #4). libxml2/6461 is sB-owned too, but its decidable:false (H6) is NOT a corpus-absence verdict -- it is tool-blindness at width-1/floating-offset, which holds on sB equally (data_realities #4).

seed-dependent tools need resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite. Verified on s4: harfbuzz/6616 has value_profile W=2 resolving + naive L=6 blocking (depth_reach scores at W=2). libxml2/6429,6461,6467 have NO rows in s4's DB (branch_ids are sB-owned; note s4's branch_id 6461 is a DIFFERENT target's branch -- cross-target branch_id collision, so do NOT score the libxml2 trio on s4). sqlite3/14658 + sqlite3/14745_h0 have VP W=2 (<3 for H2) -> honest seed-starved residue (H5) (data_realities #5).
priors_kept: vp_cmpmap_distance_closure_fixed_operand (KEEP, value_distance_reached, decidable:true) -- re-validates lcms x4 + sqlite3/14408, unchanged.
vp_admits_token_dense_corpus (KEEP, struct token-density, decidable:true) -- re-validates the SQL-grammar subset (sqlite3/14503,14588,14745_h0).
vp_admits_larger_valid_structure (KEEP, joint_necessity size/tag, decidable:true) -- re-validates harfbuzz/5302_h0.
vp_cmpmap_iteration_depth_reach (KEEP, depth_reach, decidable:true) -- re-validates libxml2/6429 + libxml2/6467_h1 + harfbuzz/6616 (confirmed round-3); libxml2/6461 removed from its member set.
vp_advantage_seed_starved (KEEP, decidable:false) -- sqlite3/14658 + sqlite3/14745_h0 honest seed-starved residue, unchanged.
new_mechanisms: vp_advantage_single_byte_floating_operand_unmeasurable (NEW, decidable:false): the ONLY still-unlabeled branch libxml2/6461 is a SINGLE-BYTE (width-1) EQUALITY to a FIXED operand (0x78='x') that FLOATS in the input (the '&#x' charref opener, no fixed head offset). Its VP-gradient mechanism is genuinely real, but NO built tool can discriminate it from blind luck: value_distance_reached collapses at width 1 (chance-prone, relaxed re-test forbidden), depth_reach finds no iteration depth past a single-byte equality (correctly scored rule_not_met), operand_enrichment needs a fixed-offset gate signature the floating opener lacks (no_gate_signature), and token-density/size are the wrong axes. Honest G3 decidable:false -- NOT a forced label.
decidable_false: vp_advantage_single_byte_floating_operand_unmeasurable (H6, decidable:false): libxml2/6461 -- width-1 floating-operand equality; every built measurement is refuted-for-this-branch or structurally blind. Honest tool-blindness inconclusive (distinct from seed-starvation). Re-decidable only via a NEW fixed-offset-agnostic population byte-enrichment tool, out of scope this round.

vp_advantage_seed_starved (H5, decidable:false): sqlite3/14658 + sqlite3/14745_h0 -- value_profile arm W=2 (<3) starves the token-density statistic, no fixed operand for distance, not font for size. Honest seed-starved inconclusive (their corpus IS on-disk, so this is starvation not absence). Candidate for a future depth_reach pass (needs only >=1 W) or rank-10 re-bisect; left inconclusive (fallback) until then.
notes:
SUPERSET / MONOTONIC: all FIVE prior hypotheses marked KEEP (vp_cmpmap_distance_closure_fixed_operand, vp_admits_token_dense_corpus, vp_admits_larger_valid_structure, vp_cmpmap_iteration_depth_reach, vp_advantage_seed_starved) are CARRIED FORWARD with UNCHANGED rules so the 13 confirmed/frozen branches re-validate without regression. The ONLY edit to a KEEP hypothesis is REMOVING libxml2/6461 from vp_cmpmap_iteration_depth_reach's member_hint_branches (it scored rule_not_met there) and moving it to a NEW honest decidable:false hypothesis H6 -- the confirmed members of H4 (libxml2/6429, libxml2/6467_h1, harfbuzz/6616) are untouched.

ROUND-3-FINAL TARGET: the single still-unlabeled branch is libxml2/6461. Diagnosis: it is structurally UNLIKE the other three H4 members. It is a SINGLE-BYTE EQUALITY (parser.c:2317 NXT(2)=='x', operand 0x78, width 1) to a FIXED operand that FLOATS in the input -- no iteration depth past it (the downstream hex parse is fixed-length, not a counter-growing loop), so depth_reach correctly did not separate it.

NOVELTY + HONEST G3 (G1/G3): I considered EVERY built tool NOT yet tried here (operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio, corpus_size_ratio, token_count) plus the already-tried ones, and each is structurally inapplicable to a width-1 floating-operand equality: (a) value_distance_reached collapses to match/no-match at width 1 (registry flags width<=2 chance-prone; the brief forbids a relaxed-threshold re-test, so re-running it with a looser rule is explicitly NOT done); (b) depth_reach has no iteration depth to measure (already rule_not_met); (c) operand_enrichment requires a FIXED-OFFSET gate signature, but '&#x' floats anywhere in an XML doc -> gate_signature returns no_gate_signature (structurally blind, matching round-2's enrichment no_gate_signature finding); (d) token_count / struct density and corpus_size_ratio measure the wrong axes (one literal byte is neither grammar-token density nor a size signature). The VP single-byte Hamming gradient IS real (the analysis confirms naive landed 'x' only 1/10 by luck) but is UNMEASURABLE by the built toolset without over-claiming. The CORRECT outcome is honest decidable:false (H6) -- I do NOT invent a rule to force a label, per G3 and the re-design rules.

WHY NOT FORCE IT: forcing libxml2/6461 into value_distance_reached at width 1 would be a relaxed-threshold re-test of an already-N/A measurement (forbidden), and forcing it into operand_enrichment would fail at the gate_signature stage (no fixed offset). Either would be a label the data cannot support. A new mechanism that could decide it -- a fixed-offset-AGNOSTIC corpus-wide population enrichment of byte 0x78 at the post-'&#' position -- is NOT in the registry and is out of scope for this round.

G2 (independent of resolve pattern): every decidable rule reads corpus CONTENT / REACH (distance, density, size, branch-line depth), never the resolve label. H6 is decidable:false so it makes no claim at all.

CROSS-SERVER (rule #4): libxml2/6461 is sB-owned, but its decidable:false is NOT a corpus-absence verdict -- it is width-1/floating-offset TOOL-BLINDNESS that holds identically on sB. So sB will also leave it inconclusive; this is a property of the measurement vocabulary, not of which server runs it. The cross-target branch_id collision caveat (s4's 6461 is a harfbuzz branch) is recorded so the arbiter keys (target,branch_id), never branch_id alone.

EXPECTED net after this round: 13 confirmed branches re-validate unchanged (lcms x4 + sqlite3/14408 via H1; sqlite3/14503,14588,14745_h0 via H2; harfbuzz/5302_h0 via H3; libxml2/6429,6467_h1,harfbuzz/6616 via H4), libxml2/6461 honestly inconclusive (H6, width-1 floating-operand tool-blindness), and the two W=2 sqlite3 deep-parse branches honestly inconclusive (H5, seed-starved). The shape is DRY: no built measurement can label libxml2/6461 without over-claiming, so an honest decidable:false is the terminal correct outcome.
